claim.content: Given finite-size effective-mass data points \\(\\{(N_{i},m^{*}(N_{i}))\\}\\) at several densities and considering the family of fits \\(m^{*}(N)=m^{*}(\\infty)+b\\,N^{-\\gamma}\\) parametrized by \\(\\gamma\\), the proposition is: adopting a fixed exponent value \\(\\gamma=3/2\\) for the extrapolations across the densities considered is justified because the resulting fits produce substantially better overall goodness-of-fit statistics as measured by the reduced \\(\\chi^{2}\\) values than alternative fixed exponents over the same data sets, and therefore using \\(\\gamma=3/2\\) yields reliable extrapolated values \\(m^{*}(\\infty)\\) for these DMC data.
